title: Quiz: Integrated Photonics
description: Test your understanding of silicon photonics, LNOI, and integrated quantum photonic platforms

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of lithium niobate on insulator (LNOI) over silicon photonics for quantum applications?

options.text: Fast electro-optic modulation (GHz+) and native chi-2 nonlinearity for SPDC

questions.explanation: LNOI offers fast electro-optic modulation (40+ GHz possible) using the Pockels effect, compared to thermal modulation in silicon (~100 kHz). Additionally, its chi-2 nonlinearity enables efficient SPDC for on-chip photon pair generation.

questions.question: In silicon photonics, what limits the speed of phase shifters in Mach-Zehnder interferometers?

options.text: Photon travel time through the circuit

questions.explanation: Standard silicon photonic phase shifters use the thermo-optic effect - heating changes the refractive index. This thermal process is slow (~100 kHz bandwidth). Faster modulation requires carrier injection/depletion, which has higher loss.

questions.question: How many MZI (Mach-Zehnder interferometer) units are needed to implement an arbitrary 8-mode unitary using a Clements or Reck decomposition?
questions.explanation: For n modes, you need n(n-1)/2 MZIs. For n=8: 8*7/2 = 28 MZIs. This applies to both Clements and Reck decompositions (they differ in circuit depth, not component count).

questions.question: What is the typical propagation loss in state-of-the-art silicon photonic waveguides at 1550 nm?
questions.explanation: State-of-the-art silicon waveguides have losses of 1-3 dB/cm. This is higher than LNOI (0.1-0.5 dB/cm) due to sidewall roughness from etching. Ultra-low loss designs can achieve better, but with larger mode area.

questions.question: What nonlinear process is used for photon pair generation in silicon photonics?
questions.explanation: Silicon has centrosymmetric crystal structure, so it lacks chi-2 nonlinearity needed for SPDC. Instead, chi-3 processes like SFWM are used: two pump photons convert to signal and idler photons conserving energy (2*omega_pump = omega_s + omega_i).

questions.question: Waveguide-integrated SNSPDs can achieve >90% on-chip detection efficiency.
questions.explanation: True! Waveguide-integrated SNSPDs have demonstrated >90% on-chip detection efficiency. The evanescent field of the waveguide mode couples to the superconducting nanowire placed on top of the waveguide, enabling efficient absorption over a short length.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the Clements mesh architecture over the Reck architecture?
questions.explanation: The Clements decomposition achieves circuit depth of n (number of modes), compared to 2n-3 for Reck. Lower depth means fewer sequential loss elements, which is crucial for maintaining high fidelity in lossy integrated circuits.

questions.question: What is V_pi for an electro-optic modulator?

options.text: The voltage that causes total reflection
The voltage required to achieve a pi phase shift
The breakdown voltage of the modulator
The operating voltage for maximum efficiency

questions.explanation: V_pi is the half-wave voltage - the voltage required to induce a pi (180 degree) phase shift through the electro-optic effect. Lower V_pi means more efficient modulation. LNOI modulators can achieve V_pi of a few volts.
